response.justification: Wei Chen has a broad technical background spanning nearly 16 years across web, embedded, mobile, and distributed systems, with genuine strength in high-throughput microservices. However, the resume contains zero evidence of the core competencies this role demands: experimentation platforms, A/B testing frameworks, feature flagging, telemetry/instrumentation infrastructure, or statistical/experimental analysis. The growth engineering domain is entirely absent. Career progression has topped out at Senior/Staff IC, well below the Principal/IC6 level required, with no demonstrated cross-org multi-workstream technical leadership or partnership with data science teams for measurement systems.
strengths: Multi-language proficiency (TypeScript, C, C++, Java, C#, DLang) maps reasonably well to the JD's required language stack
Hands-on experience with high-throughput distributed microservices at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby, processing millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency async I/O
Consistent pattern of open-source ownership, public speaking, and community knowledge-sharing that signals engineering depth and credibility
concerns: No evidence whatsoever of experimentation platforms, A/B testing frameworks, feature flagging, rollout automation, or bias/guardrail systems — the core technical surface of this role
No telemetry, metrics infrastructure, or experimental statistical analysis experience; high data aptitude required by JD is entirely undemonstrated

Career seniority (Senior/Staff IC) is two levels below Principal/IC6, with no record of driving multi-workstream cross-org initiatives or translating ambiguous business goals into measurable growth systems

key_factors.factor: No telemetry or metrics infrastructure background
